answer:
\boxed{A}

The study shows a statistically significant reduction in the prevalence of complex regional pain syndrome (CRPS-I) in patients receiving vitamin C compared to placebo (2.4% vs. 10.1%, *p* = 0.002). Higher doses of vitamin C (500 mg and 1500 mg) demonstrated stronger protective effects with lower relative risks. Thus, vitamin C appears effective in preventing CRPS-I in this population.
